volumeTitle: Volume I: Publilius Syrus. Elegies on Maecenas. Grattius. Calpurnius Siculus. Laus Pisonis. Einsiedeln Eclogues. Aetna
label: Einsiedeln Eclogues (Section 9.1 - Section 10.30)

sections.label: Section 9.1
sections.original: te , Moeri , pedes ? an , quo via ducit , in urbem ?
sections.translation: Say whither , Moeris ?— Make you for the town ,

sections.label: Section 9.2
sections.original: O Lycida , vivi pervenimus , advena nostri
sections.translation: or on what errand bent ?

sections.label: Section 9.3
sections.original: ( quod numquam veriti sumus ) ut possessor agelli
sections.translation: O Lycidas ,

sections.label: Section 9.4
sections.original: diceret : “ Haec mea sunt ; veteres migrate coloni !”
sections.translation: we have lived to see , what never yet we feared ,

sections.label: Section 9.5
sections.original: nunc victi , tristes , quoniam Fors omnia versat ,
sections.translation: an interloper own our little farm ,

sections.label: Section 9.6
sections.original: hos illi — quod nec vertat bene — mittimus haedos .
sections.translation: and say , “ Be off , you former husbandmen !

sections.label: Section 9.7
sections.original: Certe equidem audieram , qua se subducere colles
sections.translation: These fields are mine .” Now , cowed and out of heart ,

sections.label: Section 9.8
sections.original: incipiunt , mollique iugum demittere clivo ,
sections.translation: since Fortune turns the whole world upside down ,

sections.label: Section 9.9
sections.original: usque ad aquam et veteres ( iam fracta cacumina ) fagos
sections.translation: we are taking him — ill luck go with the same !—

sections.label: Section 9.10
sections.original: omnia carminibus vestrum servasse Menalcan .
sections.translation: these kids you see .

sections.label: Section 9.11
sections.original: Audieras , et fama fuit ; sed carmina tantum
sections.translation: But surely I had heard

sections.label: Section 9.12
sections.original: nostra valent , Lycida , tela inter Martia , quantum
sections.translation: that where the hills first draw from off the plain ,

sections.label: Section 9.13
sections.original: Chaonias dicunt aquila veniente columbas .
sections.translation: and the high ridge with gentle slope descends ,

sections.label: Section 9.14
sections.original: quod nisi me quacumque novas incidere lites
sections.translation: down to the brook-side and the broken crests

sections.label: Section 9.15
sections.original: ante Sinistra cava monuisset ab ilice cornix ,
sections.translation: of yonder veteran beeches , all the land

sections.label: Section 9.16
sections.original: nec tuus hic Moeris , nec viveret ipse Menalcas .
sections.translation: was by the songs of your Menalcas saved .

sections.label: Section 9.17
sections.original: Heu , cadit in quemquam tantum scelus ? Heu , tua nobis
sections.translation: Heard it you had , and so the rumour ran ,

sections.label: Section 9.18
sections.original: paene simul tecum solatia rapta , Menalca ?
sections.translation: but ' mid the clash of arms , my Lycidas ,

sections.label: Section 9.19
sections.original: quis caneret nymphas ; quis humum florentibus herbis
sections.translation: our songs avail no more than , as ' tis said ,

sections.label: Section 9.20
sections.original: spargeret , aut viridi fontes induceret umbra ?
sections.translation: doves of Dodona when an eagle comes .

sections.label: Section 9.21
sections.original: vel quae sublegi tacitus tibi carmina nuper ,
sections.translation: Nay , had I not , from hollow ilex-bole

sections.label: Section 9.22
sections.original: cum te ad delicias ferres , Amaryllida , nostras ?
sections.translation: warned by a raven on the left , cut short

sections.label: Section 9.23
sections.original: Tityre , dum redeo — brevis est via — pasce capellas ,
sections.translation: the rising feud , nor I , your Moeris here ,

sections.label: Section 9.24
sections.original: et potum pastas age , Tityre , et inter agendum
sections.translation: no , nor Menalcas , were alive to-day .

sections.label: Section 9.25
sections.original: occursare capro , cornu ferit ille , caveto .
sections.translation: Alack ! could any of so foul a crime

sections.label: Section 9.26
sections.original: Immo haec , quae Varo necdum perfecta canebat :
sections.translation: be guilty ? Ah ! how nearly , thyself ,

sections.label: Section 9.27
sections.original: “ Vare , tuum nomen , superet modo Mantua nobis —
sections.translation: reft was the solace that we had in thee ,

sections.label: Section 9.28
sections.original: Mantua , vae miserae nimium vicina Cremonae —
sections.translation: Menalcas ! Who then of the Nymphs had sung ,

sections.label: Section 9.29
sections.original: cantantes sublime ferent ad sidera cycni .”
sections.translation: or who with flowering herbs bestrewn the ground ,

sections.label: Section 9.30
sections.original: Sic tua Cyrneas fugiant examina taxos ;
sections.translation: and o ' er the fountains drawn a leafy veil ?—

sections.label: Section 10.1
sections.original: Extremum hunc , Arethusa , mihi concede laborem :
sections.translation: This now , the very latest of my toils ,

sections.label: Section 10.2
sections.original: pauca meo Gallo , sed quae legat ipsa Lycoris ,
sections.translation: vouchsafe me , Arethusa ! needs must I

sections.label: Section 10.3
sections.original: carmina sunt dicenda . neget quis carmina Gallo ?
sections.translation: sing a brief song to Gallus — brief , but yet

sections.label: Section 10.4
sections.original: sic tibi , cum fluctus subterlabere Sicanos ,
sections.translation: such as Lycoris ' self may fitly read .

sections.label: Section 10.5
sections.original: Doris amara suam non intermisceat undam .
sections.translation: Who would not sing for Gallus ? So , when thou

sections.label: Section 10.6
sections.original: incipe ; sollicitos Galli dicamus amores ,
sections.translation: beneath Sicanian billows glidest on ,

sections.label: Section 10.7
sections.original: dum tenera attondent simae virgulta capellae .
sections.translation: may Doris blend no bitter wave with thine ,

sections.label: Section 10.8
sections.original: non canimus surdis ; respondent omnia silvae .
sections.translation: begin ! The love of Gallus be our theme ,

sections.label: Section 10.9
sections.original: Quae nemora , aut qui vos saltus habuere , puellae
sections.translation: and the shrewd pangs he suffered , while , hard by ,

sections.label: Section 10.10
sections.original: Naides , indigno cum Gallus amore peribat ?
sections.translation: the flat-nosed she-goats browse the tender brush .

sections.label: Section 10.11
sections.original: nam neque Parnasi vobis iuga , nam neque Pindi
sections.translation: We sing not to deaf ears ; no word of ours

sections.label: Section 10.12
sections.original: ulla moram fecere , neque Aoniae Aganippe .
sections.translation: but the woods echo it . What groves or lawns

sections.label: Section 10.13
sections.original: Illum etiam lauri , etiam flevere myricae .
sections.translation: held you , ye Dryad-maidens , when for love —

sections.label: Section 10.14
sections.original: Pinifer illum etiam sola sub rupe iacentem
sections.translation: love all unworthy of a loss so dear —

sections.label: Section 10.15
sections.original: Maenalus , et gelidi fleverunt saxa Lycaei .
sections.translation: Gallus lay dying ? for neither did the slopes

sections.label: Section 10.16
sections.original: Stant et oves circum ;— nostri nec poenitet illas ,
sections.translation: of Pindus or Parnassus stay you then ,

sections.label: Section 10.17
sections.original: nec te poeniteat pecoris , divine poeta ;—
sections.translation: no , nor Aonian Aganippe . Him

sections.label: Section 10.18
sections.original: et formosus ovis ad flumina pavit Adonis ;
sections.translation: even the laurels and the tamarisks wept ;

sections.label: Section 10.19
sections.original: venit et upilio ; tardi venere subulci ;
sections.translation: for him , outstretched beneath a lonely rock ,

sections.label: Section 10.20
sections.original: uvidus hiberna venit de glande Menalcas .
sections.translation: wept pine-clad Maenalus , and the flinty crags

sections.label: Section 10.21
sections.original: Omnes “ Unde amor iste ” rogant “ tibi ?” Venit Apollo :
sections.translation: of cold Lycaeus . The sheep too stood around —

sections.label: Section 10.22
sections.original: “ Galle , quid insanis ?” inquit ; “ tua cura Lycoris
sections.translation: of us they feel no shame , poet divine ;

sections.label: Section 10.23
sections.original: perque nives alium perque horrida castra secuta est .”
sections.translation: nor of the flock be thou ashamed : even fair

sections.label: Section 10.24
sections.original: Venit et agresti capitis Silvanus honore ,
sections.translation: Adonis by the rivers fed his sheep —

sections.label: Section 10.25
sections.original: florentis ferulas et grandia lilia quassans .
sections.translation: came shepherd too , and swine-herd footing slow ,

sections.label: Section 10.26
sections.original: Pan deus Arcadiae venit , quem vidimus ipsi
sections.translation: and , from the winter-acorns dripping-wet

sections.label: Section 10.27
sections.original: sanguineis ebuli bacis minioque rubentem .
sections.translation: Menalcas . All with one accord exclaim :

sections.label: Section 10.28
sections.original: “ Ecquis erit modus ?” inquit ; “ Amor non talia curat ;
sections.translation: “ From whence this love of thine ?” Apollo came ;

sections.label: Section 10.29
sections.original: nec lacrimis crudelis Amor , nec gramina rivis ,
sections.translation: “ Gallus , art mad ?” he cried , “ thy bosom ' s care

sections.label: Section 10.30
sections.original: nec cytiso saturantur apes , nec fronde capellae .”
sections.translation: another love is following .” Therewithal
